channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: New York Liberty basketball team honored with ticker-tape parade in Canyon of Heroes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jonquel Jones, Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu and their New York teammates were showered with confetti as the Liberty celebrated winning the WNBA title in the Canyon of Heroes in downtown Manhattan. New York Sen. Chuck Schumer, Gov.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cowboys kicker Brandon Aubrey gets jury duty but is expected to play at Niners
item.description: The Dallas Cowboys expect Brandon Aubrey to be available in San Francisco after missing the first two practices of the week when the kicker was selected for jury duty.
item.dc:creator: Schuyler Dixon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cardinals call audible on game plan for cheerleaders due to new seating
item.description: The Arizona Cardinals changed their game plan for their cheerleading squad, and alumni took notice, appearing to be upset with the move.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Soccer match between Team USA and Mexico to take place in North County
item.description: The very first International Indoor Soccer Arena (IISA) match at Frontwave Arena takes place this weekend in Oceanside.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees-Dodgers iconic moments: Reggie Jackson becomes Mr. October
item.description: Reggie Jackson hit three home runs on three consecutive swings of his bat to clinch the Yankees' first World Series in 15 years back in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dolphins' Tua Tagovailoa takes firm stance on Guardian Cap use
item.description: Miami Dolphins star quarterback Tua Tagovailoa made clear his stance on whether he would wear a Guardian Cap over his helmet for the rest of the season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to win World Series tickets for game at Yankee Stadium
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) --- If you don't have $1,000 to treat yourself to a World Series game, the Yankees are giving away two tickets to Game 3 in the Bronx on Monday night. Fans can enter the sweepstakes via text or by filling out an entry form online. The entry period ends at 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Tony La Russa sizes up World Series matchup, works on new pet project
item.description: Hall of Fame manager Tony La Russa, who is launching a new animal-rescue effort, sees the upcoming World Series as a toss up between the Dodgers and Yankees.
item.dc:creator: Cam Inman, The Mercury News

item.title: CFB picks for Week 9: Washington and Colorado win outright, Vanderbilt covers (again) as BYU’s run comes to an end
item.description: It's time for Washington to win a Big Ten road game, especially with Indiana missing its starting QB.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Fernando Valenzuela, Mexican-born pitcher whose feats for Dodgers fueled 'Fernandomania,' dies at 63
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died. He was 63.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63, just days before former team faces Yankees in World Series
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ottawa shuts out Utah Hockey Club, 4-0
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was an eight-minute blitz from which the Utah Hockey Club could not receover. Anton Forsberg made 31 saves, Drake Batherson scored his third goal in two games and the Ottawa Senators won their first road game of the season, 4-0 victory over Utah HC Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Blackhawks captain not pleased after Canucks score 4 first-period goals in 6-3 win over Chicago
item.description: Danton Heinen scored two of Vancouver's four first-period goals, and the Canucks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 6-3 on Tuesday night for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: BUCS BONUS: Chris Godwin to undergo surgery, out for rest of regular season
item.description: Godwin, the league leader in receptions through the first seven weeks of the season, will likely miss the rest of the 2024 season.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York Liberty win first WNBA championship, beating Minnesota 67-62 in OT
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As confetti fell and Frank Sinatra’s “New York, New York” blared through the arena, the New York Liberty celebrated the end of a long odyssey.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers vs. Yankees in the World Series? It's more common than you think
item.description: The Fall Classic is set, and the matchup is a classic all right. The Los Angeles Dodgers clinched the National League pennant on Sunday, closing out the New York Mets in six games in the NL Championship Series. That means that baseball fans are getting a dream matchup in the World Series.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Photo Gallery: NL Champs! Dodgers finish Mets to advance to World Series
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are heading to the 2024 World Series. With a 10-5 home win over the Mets on Sunday, the Dodgers claimed their 25th National League pennant, beating New York 4 games to 2 in the best of seven series.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Moody agrees to 3-year, $39M extension with Warriors: report
item.description: (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors and guard Moses Moody have agreed to a multi-year contract extension, ESPN's Shams Charania posted Sunday evening on X. The extension is reportedly for three years and $39 million, which is an average annual value of about $13 million.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: CMU def. New Mexico Highlands 21-10 in homecoming game
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—In the second quarter, with the game tied at zero, the Cowboys sought to break the deadlock. Joey Cave handed the ball off to Mike Jones, who found space after strong blocking from his teammates.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Kyle Larson opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs as the title favorite
item.description: Kyle Larson has been lucky of late in Las Vegas, where he opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs on top of the Cup Series standings and the favorite to win Sunday's race.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Guardians starting Tanner Bibee in Game 5 against Yankees after short outing earlier in ALCS
item.description: Tanner Bibee is getting a second crack at the Yankees in the AL Championship Series. His first one didn't go so well. The right-hander will start for the Cleveland Guardians in Game 5 on Saturday after he was pulled from Game 2 in the second inning by manager Stephen Vogt.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5
